patterns: [mpm_prefork:error] *.* AH00161: server reached MaxRequestWorkers setting *.* consider raising the MaxRequestWorkers setting
keystone.issue:
Error: Exception KeyError

This error indicates that there aren't enough processes for keystone to handle the requests

Recovery steps:

1. Restore the UC backup taken before starting Scale In/Out
2. Edit the following parameters processes=24 threads=8 in following files on UC:

/var/lib/config-data/puppet-generated/keystone/etc/httpd/conf.d/10-keystone_wsgi_admin.conf
/var/lib/config-data/puppet-generated/keystone/etc/httpd/conf.d/10-keystone_wsgi_main.conf

5. Restart keystone and keystone_cron containers on the UC
6. Retry scale In/Out

mistral.issue:
tripleo-admin user password has expired.

1. Verify whether password for user tripleo-admin has expired or not:

salt "*" cmd.run "sudo chage -l tripleo-admin" 

2. The password shall never expire. 

Set it like this:

salt "*" cmd.run "sudo chage -M -1 tripleo-admin"

tripleo-admin user password has expired.

1. Verify whether password for user tripleo-admin has expired or not:

salt "*" cmd.run "sudo chage -l tripleo-admin" 

2. The password shall never expire. 

Set it like this:

salt "*" cmd.run "sudo chage -M -1 tripleo-admin"

patterns: FAILED - RETRYING: wait for all osd to be up
mistral.issue: The Disk might failing causing osd to be down, please check for disk health

patterns: Stderr: u'Error: Unable to establish IPMI v2 / RMCP+ session\n'.: ProcessExecutionError: Unexpected error while running command.
ironic.issue:
In a case where a compute/storage node has failed and cant be repaired(server is dead and ipmi unreachable) please use the following procedure:

1.steps:
1.	Set the node to maintenance mode:

openstack baremetal node maintenance set storage --reason "dead"

2.	Delete the node from ironic:

*To get the uuid run openstack baremetal node list and identify the desired node id
 
      openstack baremetal node delete <NODE UUID>

3.	Run the following command to continue the scale in operation:

*To get the uuid run openstack server list and identify the desired node id

openstack cbis node remove <Nova node ID>

patterns: failed to connect *.* to the hypervisor
scale-in/out.issue:
The error is mentioned in this bugzilla https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1684286

It says: Deploy fails when node has wrong clock


Solution:

Set date and time in BIOS as close as possible to the current one,

then do sudo sgdisk -Z /dev/sda

patterns: docker exec barbican_worker id -g barbican *.* Error response from daemon: Container *.* is not running
scale-in/out.issue:
Error response from daemon: Container is not running

The error caused by barbican API containers which are in restarting state.

Recovery Steps:

1.Delete barbican_api container:

docker rm -f barbican_api

2.Recreate barbican API and rerun post install:

paunch apply --file /var/lib/tripleo-config/hashed-docker-container-startup-config-step_3.json --config-id tripleo_step3

Please check Rabbitmq cluster status using the following command on each controller:

sudo docker exec -it `sudo docker ps | grep rabbit | awk '{print $1}'` rabbitmqctl cluster_status

If rabbitmq is down or out of cluster on one of the controllers, reboot the controller

Error: Scale out/upgrade failed due to external signed cert

Scale in/out or upgrade fails in case the customer utilizing External Key.(also known as "User provided TLS Certificates and Key")

Recovery steps:

Before any scale in/out or Upgrade,
 user must revert his external key by running again "Update of TLS certificates" without turning on the option "User provided TLS Certificates and Key" on CBIS manager.
(section "Platform Secrets Update")

This will revert back to self-sign in option and will allow you to do the upgrade/stack update without any issue.

ansible hosts execution has failed
please run the following:

1. python /usr/share/cbis/undercloud/tools/configure_etc_hosts.py -e 
to get /etc/hosts file to apply to new compute hosts.
python /usr/share/cbis/undercloud/tools/configure_etc_hosts.py -s
to get /etc/ssh/ssh_known_hosts to new compute hosts.

Error caused due to lack of resources

Troubleshooting steps:

1. Verify the Undercloud resources requirement is fulfilled as stated in CBIS Operation manual:"When scaling out, please assign the following memory / vCPU according to the number of computes: 60+ computes: 20 vCPUs and 90% of the Undercloud Physical Server memory".
2. Monitoring Undercloud heat engine is consuming a lot of memory, if heat engine consume large amount of memory, the processes may be blocked and cause this problem. If this is the case, decrease number of heat engine workers (num_engine_workers=32 in /etc/heat/heat.conf) from 32 to 24.
3. If the problem still exist, decrease forks from 50 to 15 or to 5 /etc/ansible/ansible.cfg.

patterns: The vm pool didn't configure on OvsCompute
scale-in/out.issue:
1. Please make sure that each "Zone" was configured with one of the multiple pools before clicking "Deploy"
2.This is a known issue which has been fixed in CBIS19A SP2 PP1

patterns: Exception caught during deployment: Unable to establish connection to *.* HTTPConnectionPool(*.*, port=*.*): Max retries exceeded with url
scale-in/out.issue:
Please verify that haproxy file has no missing endpoints,

Example:

salt "*ontrol*" cmd.run "sudo grep -A4 'listen keystone_admin' /var/lib/config-data/puppet-generated/haproxy/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg "
controller-1:
 listen keystone_admin
 bind 10.7.111.8:13357 transparent ssl crt /etc/pki/tls/private/overcloud_endpoint.pem    <===== line that disappears
 bind 172.31.0.14:35357 transparent
 mode http
